macro_summary: Equities closed Friday near record highs as the S&P 500 added 0.1% and the Nasdaq rose 0.9%, buoyed by a rebound in semis and steady oil prices after Trump said the US would 'guide' ships through the Strait of Hormuz. The Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50%–3.75% in April, citing energy‑price uncertainty, while the ECB also held. Big‑tech earnings continued to diverge: Microsoft’s Azure jumped 40% but its OpenAI revenue cap adds a new structural risk.
commodities_note: WTI eased to $101.81/bbl (−0.13%) as the naval‑escort announcement calmed immediate supply fears, though analysts still flag a $100–160 range. Gold slipped 0.74% to $4,610. The US 10‑year yield ticked down to 4.38%, staying above the 4.20% level that keeps pressure on Singapore banks and rate‑sensitive tech valuations.

The next catalyst is the dual‑channel NIM reset — US10Y below 4.20% or a dovish MAS — but neither is imminent. Reassess at S$56.40.

Entry zone $7.30–7.64, stop below $7.30. The thesis rests on commercial fleet scale and Uber/Grab partnerships; the ADR/delisting risk is priced. Fragility: single catalyst (robotaxi licensing) — keep starter sizing. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation. ACCUMULATE — starter position.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain under NIM compression as US10Y 4.38% stays above the 4.20% reset and MAS kept the S$NEER slope unchanged. OCBC (HOLD) and UOB (HOLD) face earnings this week that could either validate the trough or extend the squeeze; DBS (CAUTION) is technically overbought and untouchable.
singapore.key_development: OCBC reports May 8, UOB May 7 — NIM trajectory and loan growth guidance will determine if any name can break out of HOLD.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX rose to 465.75 (+1.7%), but the cluster is overwhelmingly CAUTION — only ASML holds a WATCH signal with a 2.1:1 R:R. NVDA, AMD, INTC, MU, TSM, AVGO, and LITE are all hard‑blocked on extreme extension. Bifurcation persists: ASML’s EUV monopoly is the lone actionable entry.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD and LITE report May 6 — both are binary events that could either justify or collapse their extreme premiums.

big_tech.summary: MSFT (WATCH) benefits from Azure’s 40% surge but faces a new OpenAI revenue cap that clouds organic demand. AMZN and GOOG are 19‑22% above their 50‑day averages — untouchable. PLTR has a 77× forward multiple and reports today; the outcome will determine if it can graduate from CAUTION.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — capex confirmed, but revenue concentration risks persist
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft’s OpenAI partnership revamp caps revenue share through 2030, shifting dependency from concentration to structural limitation.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE’s May 6 print confirms AI optics revenue above $950M run‑rate and COHR pulls back 5‑7%.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a 10% pullback from current levels following CPI on May 12 — data‑center budget barometer.

active_risks: Iran‑Hormuz crisis: US naval escort plan eases immediate supply blockage but shipping remains disrupted; WTI $101.81.
US‑China tech trade war: Export controls on jet engines and semiconductor equipment escalate; China retaliates with rare‑earth restrictions.
Federal Reserve hold: Rates at 3.50‑3.75% with no cut on horizon; energy inflation keeps hawkish bias.
Singapore NIM compression: US10Y 4.38% and strong SGD keep dual‑channel margin pressure on banks.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101.81 supports defense (PLTR) while compressing consumer discretionary and logistics margins. Semis remain extended as AI capex sustains high valuations, but only ASML offers a workable entry. Singapore banks are pinned — DBS is overbought, OCBC and UOB await earnings for any NIM relief signal.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced US would 'guide' ships through Strait of Hormuz; Iran state media claim of striking a US warship was denied by Central Command.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries in extended semis or pre‑earnings names. Reassess after PLTR, AMD, LITE, UOB, and OCBC earnings this week.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 16 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension; five earnings binaries this week will decide if the cluster can shed CAUTION.
